refinement_sections.content: The client wants to know if the new wellness programs help reduce stress. We need to check if the survey data shows a clear difference in stress levels before and after the programs.

We need to define what we mean by 'stress levels' and how they were measured in the survey. Also, we should identify which departments are included in the survey and if there are any important differences between them.

We need to decide how to analyze the data. This could involve looking at the average stress levels before and after the programs and comparing them. We might need to use a t-test or ANOVA if there are multiple departments.
We should consider if the data is normally distributed and if the variances are similar across departments. This will help us choose the right test.
After conducting the analysis, we will summarize whether the wellness programs appear to significantly reduce stress levels based on the statistical results.
